text: In recent weeks it has been good to see a number of prisons beginning to
resume services and Bible studies, and to start to have opportunities to
open up God’s Word with groups of prisoners once again. With limits on
numbers attending, and other prisons yet to re-start these activities, we
are also continuing to supply weekly written messages and other material,
and it has been encouraging to hear of ways these are still being used.
One of our chaplains has recently had contact with Dwayne, a prisoner
who has an extremely difficult home situation and seems to feel safest in
prison. Our chaplain was able to visit him on the anniversary of the death
of his twin and Dwayne was very grateful for that support. He says he
does not believe in God, but is happy to receive the weekly messages, so
we pray that something of what he reads and the contact with our
chaplain will be used to speak to him.
It was also good recently to hear from Ivan, a Hungarian prisoner we have
mentioned previously. He wrote thanking us for the regular messages and
letting us know that how much he was enjoying an Emmaus
happy I got that course, Correspondence course we had sent saying, “I’m
The Bible Speaks. It is very useful for me and helped me to understand so
many things. Now when I read the Bible I understand everything better.”
As well as studying himself, Ivan has also been sharing what he is learning
with other Christian prisoners he sees in the exercise yard.
Prisoners also continue to receive Gideon New Testaments and for many
of them, they are reading the Scriptures for the first time. One of our
often chaplains has found it a real encouragement to see this, saying “How
do we get asked for Bibles in our ordinary day-to-day lives outside of
Please pray that the Lord will use His Word to speak to many. prison!”
Aled (Ex-offender) – Give thanks that Aled continues to get on well in the
United States. Please pray for him as he seeks to rebuild relationships with
HMP Birmingham (West Midlands) – Give thanks for opportunities to run
services here in coming months and for encouraging contact with
individual prisoners. Please pray for three men who are currently reading
Chaplaincy– Please pray for chaplains undertaking one-to-one meetings
with prisoners with complex needs and challenging behaviour, that they
will be given much wisdom and sensitivity.
Pray particularly for one such prisoner, Malcolm. He is due to be released
soon, but has a complicated home life and is struggling with self-harm.
Cheshire Prayer & Support Group – Give thanks for George & Jackie
Gibson who have given much time to Daylight’s work over a number of
years, originally being involved in prison ministry in Surrey and then more
recently coordinating the Cheshire Prayer & Support Group. Please pray
for them as they step back from the work and for Paul Forrest as he takes
on the running of the group, coordinating services in HMP Thorn Cross.
HMP Deerbolt (County Durham) – Give thanks that Bible study groups are
due to recommence on a phased return. This will initially be limited to two
wings being able to meet together, which will mean the introduction of a
Please pray for the recruitment of a new Managing Chaplain and for
John as he helps to cover extra hours in the meantime.
HMP Holme House (County Durham) – Give thanks that the lifting of
restrictions means that John is now able to return to his work in this
prison. Please pray that he will be given wisdom in deciding how best to
Jim – Give thanks that Jim was recently encouraged by reading the book
of Romans. He has now been moved to another prison and his trial is
approaching. Please pray that he will remember what he has learned,
continue to read his Bible and find Christian support and encouragement
as he prepares to go to court. on next Continued page...
HMP Oakwood – Give thanks for opportunities to be involved in running
services here once again. Please pray that prisoners will be keen to come
Solent Prayer & Support Group – Give thanks that the group have been
able to meet to pray together via Zoom recently and for the opportunity
for a few of the team to run a service in a local prison on 6th June for
the first time since the pandemic began. Please pray for the men who
HMP Sudbury (Derbyshire) – Give thanks that it has been possible to
re-start Bible studies here and for Simon, a prisoner who has been
encouraging others to come along to these. Please pray that the studies
will be an encouragement to the men who attend.
HMP Swansea (South Wales) – Give thanks for the opportunity to return
to running services here last month. Please pray that the messages
preached will be used in the hearts and lives of many.
HMP Wayland (Norfolk) – Please pray for S, a Christian prisoner who has
recently begun an Emmaus Bible School correspondence course and for P,
a new prisoner who has expressed an interest in Christian things. He has
a history of addiction and is struggling with withdrawal symptoms, but
an established Christian on his wing is trying to help and witness to him.
Please also pray that nothing will stand in the way of resuming Bible
studies and Sunday services now that the prison is clear of coronavirus
infection and for the recruitment of a new governor, that the person
appointed will be someone who will work well with the chaplaincy
West Yorkshire Prayer & Support Group – Give thanks that one of the
local prisons has invited the team to a weekly Free Church Worship Time
and a Meet & Chill Session, with just one member of the team attending
each time. Please pray that they will have opportunities to speak to
individual prisoners about the Lord and will be given wisdom as to what
A number of the chaplains have asked for prayer for additional strength
at this time, as things begin to open up, particularly with regard to
organising meetings and services in the very near future.
